info.title: Renaiss OS Index — Public API
info.description:
Read-only JSON over the Renaiss OS Index data. Every endpoint is a `GET`
and responses carry permissive CORS, so you can call them straight from the
browser via **Try it out** below.

### Rate limits

| Tier | Limit |
| --- | --- |
| Anonymous (no key) | **10 requests / day** per IP |
| API key (partner) | **10,000 requests / day** per key |
| The Renaiss index website | unlimited (exempt) |

Every response carries `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, and
`X-RateLimit-Reset` (unix seconds). Going over returns `429` with a
`Retry-After` header.

### Authentication (optional)

The API is public — no key is needed to try it. To lift the anonymous daily
limit, send your key as two headers: `X-Api-Key` (the `rk_…` id) and
`X-Api-Secret` (the `rsk_…` secret). Keys are self-serve: sign in with
Renaiss on the index site and, once your email or wallet is whitelisted,
create up to 3 keys under **Settings → API access**.

**Finding card slugs:** start at `/v1/search?q=...`. Each result includes an
`href` of the form `/card/{game}/{set}/{card}` — feed those three segments
into the `/v1/cards/{game}/{set}/{card}` endpoints.

Full index detail for one game, including its ranked constituents.

get.description: The card's daily fair-market value — the reference-price methodology (mean of completed sales over a trailing window) evaluated once per day (the chart line) — plus, for each day, that day's sales broken down by source (count + that day's mean), for the chart's hover. Powers the card price chart.

get.description: Grade-agnostic view of a card: its identity plus every grading company / grade we track for it. Here `card` is the grade-less slug (`{number}-{name}`) — drop the `-{company}-{grade}` suffix from a card `href`.

parameters.name: card
parameters.description: Grade-less card slug (`{number}-{name}`) — a card `href`'s final segment with the `-{company}-{grade}` suffix removed.

get.description: Every card we track in a set, keyed by the set slug (the 2nd path segment of a card `href`).

parameters.name: game
parameters.description: Game slug.

parameters.name: set
parameters.description: Set slug — the 2nd path segment of a card `href` returned by `/v1/search` (`/card/{game}/{set}/{card}`).

404.description: Unknown game, or set not found.

get.description: Look up a graded card by certification number (PSA / CGC / BGS). Cached-first: returns the card plus the FMV for the cert's grade. On a cache miss it runs a live on-demand valuation — available to all callers (a cache hit is free; only a genuine miss runs the pipeline). The per-IP rate limit bounds how often any one caller triggers a miss; send an API key (`X-Api-Key` + `X-Api-Secret`) for a higher ceiling. Always returns 200 for a parseable cert — inspect `found`.

parameters.name: cert
parameters.description: Certification number, optionally prefixed by the grader. E.g. `PSA149595098`, `149595098`, `CGC1234567`.

400.description: Could not parse a cert number from the input.

get.description:
Server-Sent Events variant of `/v1/graded/{cert}`. Streams the on-demand
valuation as it runs, so a client can show live progress instead of blocking
on the full pipeline. Consume with `EventSource`.

The `text/event-stream` body carries named events:
- `event: progress` — a `PipelineProgress` object as each stage begins
  (`cert_lookup` → `identify` → `enrich` → `find_item` → `cache_check` →
  `match` → `crawl` → `fmv` → `done`).
- `event: result` — the terminal `GradedLookup` (inspect `found`).
- `event: failed` — `{ error, detail }` on an unparseable cert or internal error.

A cache hit emits a single `result` with no `progress`. A miss runs the live
compute for any caller (including anonymous), bounded by the per-IP rate limit;
send an API key (`X-Api-Key` + `X-Api-Secret`) for a higher ceiling.

parameters.name: cert
parameters.description: Certification number, optionally prefixed by the grader. E.g. `PSA149595098`, `149595098`, `CGC1234567`.

200.description: SSE stream: `progress` events (each a `PipelineProgress`) followed by a terminal `result` (a `GradedLookup`) or `failed`.

schema.description: Raw SSE frames. `progress` data is a PipelineProgress; `result` data is a GradedLookup.

post.description:
Value a graded card from a PHOTO instead of a cert number — upload the slab /
card image and the AI reads the card identity and grading label off it, then the
same FMV pipeline prices it. Streams progress over SSE exactly like
`/v1/graded/{cert}/stream`; consume with `EventSource` / a fetch reader.

Every call is a live, paid pipeline run (there is no cache key for an arbitrary
photo), so the compute rate limit is enforced BEFORE the stream opens; send an API
key (`X-Api-Key` + `X-Api-Secret`) for a higher ceiling.

The `text/event-stream` body carries named events:
- `event: progress` — a `PipelineProgress` object as each stage runs.
- `event: result` — the terminal `GradedLookup` (inspect `found`).
- `event: failed` — `{ error, detail }` when the image is not a readable card
  (`image_unreadable`), the upload fails, or on an internal error.

file.description: The card or slab photo (JPEG / PNG / WebP / AVIF / HEIC, ≤ 15 MB).

200.description: SSE stream: `progress` events (each a `PipelineProgress`) followed by a terminal `result` (a `GradedLookup`) or `failed`.

schema.description: Raw SSE frames. `progress` data is a PipelineProgress; `result` data is a GradedLookup.

400.description: Bad upload — wrong content-type, missing "file" part, not an image, or larger than 15 MB.

category.description: Source category (web3 grouping axis): public = crawled marketplaces, blockchain = third-party on-chain marketplaces, renaiss = Renaiss-owned sources, partner = partner shops, internal = non-marketplace origins. Additive to the legacy `bucket` field.

ok.description: Always true when the service is up.

db.description: Whether a database connection (DATABASE_URL) is configured.

rateLimit.description: Whether a rate-limit store (Upstash) is configured. When false, all rate limiting fails open.

internalAuth.description: Whether the server-only internal token is configured (the non-spoofable trust signal).

ApiKeyId.name: X-Api-Key
ApiKeyId.description: Your public key id (`rk_…`). Pair with X-Api-Secret.

ApiKeySecret.name: X-Api-Secret
ApiKeySecret.description: Your secret (`rsk_…`, shown once at creation). Pair with X-Api-Key.
